data.tgt_id: Risk of Developing Coronary Heart Disease
data.content:
correlation,independent effect	Cholesterol Level
Risk of Developing Coronary Heart Disease
A 1% difference in cholesterol is associated with a 2.66% difference in risk, regardless of the blood pressure level.

relationship description,statistical modeling	Discriminant Functions
Framingham Population
Discriminant functions were used to describe the relationships within the Framingham population.

data.tgt_id: Risk of Developing Coronary Heart Disease
data.content:
correlation,independent effect	Blood Pressure
Risk of Developing Coronary Heart Disease
A 1% difference in blood pressure is associated with a 4.62% difference in risk, regardless of the cholesterol level.

risk comparison,statistical data	5th Percentile
Cholesterol Level
The 5th percentile of cholesterol is used to establish a baseline for comparing risk differences against the 95th percentile.

quantitative dependence,risk determinant	CHD Population
Serum Cholesterol
The risk of developing CHD is quantitatively dependent on serum cholesterol levels.

relative increase,risk ratio	95th Percentile
Cholesterol Level
The risk difference associated with the 95th percentile of cholesterol is compared to the 5th percentile.

quantitative dependence,risk determinant	CHD Population
Systolic Blood Pressure
The risk of developing CHD is quantitatively dependent on systolic blood pressure levels.

interaction effect,multiplicative relationship	Blood Pressure
Cholesterol Level
The effect of simultaneous differences in cholesterol and blood pressure on risk is multiplicative.

mathematical derivation,shared function	Log (Blood Pressure - 75)
Log10 Cholesterol
Both log10 cholesterol and log (blood pressure - 75) are variables used in calculating model coefficients.

coefficient determination,statistical basis	CHD Population
Variances
The coefficients are computed based on the variances of the CHD and NCHD populations.

correlation,quantification	4.62% Difference
Blood Pressure
A 1% increase in systolic blood pressure is associated with a 4.62% difference in risk.

Determinant,Joint Dependence	Cholesterol
Risk
The calculated risk is dependent on the level of cholesterol.

coefficient determination,statistical basis	CHD Population
Correlation Coefficient
The coefficients are computed based on the correlation coefficient between the variables.

risk comparison,statistical data	5th Percentile
Blood Pressure
The 5th percentile of blood pressure is used to establish a baseline for comparing risk differences against the 95th percentile.

approximation,statistical method	CHD Population
Confidence Limits
Confidence limits provide approximations for the coefficients used in the CHD risk model.

Dependence,Statistical Modeling	Bivariate Normal Model
Risk
The Bivariate Normal Model summarizes the joint dependence of risk on cholesterol and blood pressure.

relative increase,risk ratio	5th Percentile
95th Percentile
The population in the upper 5% of either variable has a risk more than 4.8 times as large as that in the lower 5%.

Association,Calculation	Equation 7
Risk
Equation 7 describes the mathematical association between risk and changes in cholesterol and blood pressure.

Unit of Measurement,Variable Definition	X1
mg/100 cc
X1 uses mg/100 cc as its unit of measurement for cholesterol.

Unit of Measurement,Variable Definition	X2
mm Hg
X2 uses mm Hg as its unit of measurement for blood pressure.

Level Independence,Methodology	Percentage Change
Risk
A percentage difference in cholesterol is associated with the same percentage difference in risk, regardless of the level.

mathematical application,probability derivation	Discriminant Functions
Risk Function P(Y)
Elementary algebraic manipulation or the application of Bayes' formula using discriminant functions is sufficient to derive the risk function P(Y).

Time Scale Conversion	6-Year Risk
L-Year Risk
The l-year risk is defined by its mathematical relationship to the 6-year risk, specifically being one-sixth of it.

Determinant,Joint Dependence	Blood Pressure
Risk
The calculated risk is dependent on the level of blood pressure.

cholesterol analysis,statistical characterization	CHD Population
Log Normal Distribution
The log normal distribution provides an excellent description for the CHD population's relationship with cholesterol.

Applicability Scope	Equation 7
L-Year Risk
The approximation used in Equation 7 holds for the l-year risk for all observed X1 and X2 values.

input variable,proportion calculation	Risk Function P(Y)
Serum Cholesterol
The value of serum cholesterol, Y, is the primary variable used to calculate the risk function P(Y).

Level Dependence,Methodology	Absolute Change
Risk
The absolute change in risk depends on the level from which the change takes place, differing based on whether the level is high or low.

Correlation,Joint Dependence	Blood Pressure
Cholesterol
The joint dependence of risk is analyzed across both cholesterol and blood pressure levels.

cholesterol analysis,statistical characterization	Log Normal Distribution
NCHD Population
The log normal distribution is applicable to characterizing the NCHD population's relationship with cholesterol.

joint distribution,multi-variable analysis	Bivariate Normal Frequency Function
CHD Population
The CHD population's data is analyzed using the Bivariate Normal Frequency Function in relation to log10 cholesterol and log10 (blood pressure – 75).

proportional estimation,statistical calculation	Proportion P
Risk Function P(Y)
The proportion P, combined with the population proportions of CHD and NCHD, is mathematically necessary for deducing the final risk function P(Y).

Comparison,Prediction	Expected Number Of CHD Cases
Risk
The expected number of CHD cases is calculated based on the overall risk model established by the variables.

affiliation,employment	Jerome Cornfield
National Heart Institute
Jerome Cornfield was associated with the National Heart Institute while conducting the research.

multivariate statistics,simultaneous measurement	Blood Pressure
Serum Cholesterol
Both serum cholesterol and blood pressure are variables considered together in the Bivariate Normal Frequency Function.

risk analysis,statistical modeling	Coronary Heart Disease
Discriminant Function Analysis
The method of Discriminant Function Analysis was used to analyze the joint dependence of CHD risk.

joint distribution,multi-variable analysis	Bivariate Normal Frequency Function
NCHD Population
The NCHD population's data is analyzed using the Bivariate Normal Frequency Function, making the variables simultaneously measurable.

data.src_id: Federation of American Societies for Experimental Biology
data.content:
contribution,publication source	Federation of American Societies for Experimental Biology
Jerome Cornfield
The paper was published through a venue associated with the Federation of American Societies for Experimental Biology.

dependence,risk factor association	Coronary Heart Disease
Serum Cholesterol
There is a dependency of the risk of CHD on the level of serum cholesterol, according to the analysis.

dependence,risk factor association	Coronary Heart Disease
Systolic Blood Pressure
There is a dependency of the risk of CHD on the level of systolic blood pressure, according to the analysis.

longitudinal study,study location	Coronary Heart Disease
Framingham, Massachusetts
The Coronary Heart Disease risk was studied in a long-term follow-up conducted in Framingham, Massachusetts.

clinical manifestation,diagnosis	Coronary Heart Disease
Myocardial Infarction
Myocardial infarction is listed as a specific form of coronary heart disease.

clinical manifestation,diagnosis	Angina Pectoris
Coronary Heart Disease
Angina pectoris is listed as a specific form of coronary heart disease.

publication context,research presentation	Coronary Heart Disease
Federation Proceedings
The findings regarding CHD were presented in the Federation Proceedings.

Treatment,Vascular Effects,blood pressure management,treatment	Calcium Antagonists
Hypertension
Calcium antagonists are effective in treating hypertension.<SEP>Calcium antagonists are effective in treating hypertension and decrease coronary vascular resistance.

pharmacological synergy,symptom reduction	Diltiazem
Verapamil
Verapamil and diltiazem both reduce myocardial oxygen demand by affecting heart rate and contractility.

referral necessity,specialty care	Cardiologist
Worsening Anginal Symptoms
Patients experiencing worsening anginal symptoms should be referred to a cardiologist.

treatment,vasospastic relief	Angina Pectoris
Calcium Antagonists
Calcium antagonists effectively treat angina pectoris and relieve vasospastic angina.

diagnosis,diagnostic hallmark	Acute MI
Electrocardiogram
The electrocardiogram serves as the hallmark diagnosis for Acute MI because of detectable characteristic wave changes.

referral necessity,specialty care	Acute Coronary Syndrome
Cardiologist
Patients presenting with Acute Coronary Syndrome should be referred to a cardiologist.

assessment,diagnostic focus	Haemodynamic Instability
Physical Examination
The physical examination should be directed towards identifying haemodynamic instability.

mechanism of action,myocardial protection	Calcium Antagonists
Coronary Artery Disease (CAD)
Calcium antagonists decrease coronary vascular resistance and increase coronary blood flow, beneficial for CAD.

medical process,treatment modality	Fibrinolytic Therapy
Reperfusion Therapy
Fibrinolytic therapy is a method of reperfusion therapy that should be initiated as early as possible.

assessment,diagnostic focus	Physical Examination
Pulmonary Congestion
The physical examination should be directed towards identifying pulmonary congestion.

Pathophysiology,Rupture	Acute Coronary Syndromes
Atherosclerotic Plaque
Acute coronary syndromes are triggered by the acute rupture of a lipid-rich atheromatous plaque.

intervention,treatment goal	Acute MI
Reperfusion Therapy
The main goal of treatment for Acute MI is to reduce irreversible myocardial tissue damage using Reperfusion Therapy.

co-management,ongoing care	Cardiologist
Primary Care Physicians
Primary care physicians and cardiologists may co-manage patients by alternating follow-up evaluations.

Assessment,Diagnosis	Coronary Artery Disease
Exercise Stress Testing (EST)
EST is a key investigative method used to detect and quantify chronic ischaemic heart disease associated with coronary artery disease.

Cascade,Trigger	Atherosclerotic Plaque
Thrombus
The rupture of the plaque exposes highly thrombogenic contents, initiating platelet aggregation and the formation of a thrombus.

Oxygen Limitation,Supply-Demand Mismatch	Coronary Blood Flow
Myocardial Ischaemia
Myocardial ischaemia results when the oxygen delivery, influenced by coronary flow, cannot meet the metabolic demands.

medical process,treatment modality	Reperfusion Therapy
Thrombolytic Therapy
Thrombolytic therapy is a potential form of reperfusion therapy.

Outcome,Severity	Acute Coronary Syndromes
Infarcation
If collateralization is insufficient or the occlusion is complete, acute coronary syndromes can result in myocardial infarction.

co-management,follow-up care	Cardiologist
Primary Care Physician
Primary care physicians and cardiologists collaborate on the follow-up care of patients.

diagnosis,indication	Acute Myocardial Infarction
ST Segment Elevation
ST segment elevation exceeding 1.0 mV is indicative of AMI.

Alternative Treatment,procedure comparison	Primary Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI)
Thrombolysis
PCI can

anticoagulant therapy,comparative effectiveness	Low Molecular Weight Heparins
Unfractionated Heparin
Treatment with low molecular weight heparins significantly reduces combined endpoints compared to unfractionated heparin.

Progression,Severity	Myocardial Ischaemia
Unstable Angina
Ischaemia is the underlying state that, under certain circumstances, leads to the diagnosis of unstable angina.

procedure sequence,risk stratification	Cardiac Catheterization
Revascularization
Revascularization should be considered following cardiac catheterization, especially if refractory ischemia is present.

data.tgt_id: Ministry of Health And Quality Of Life/Mauritius Institute Of Health/World Health Organisation
data.content:
clinical monitoring,guideline source	Hypertension
Ministry of Health And Quality Of Life/Mauritius Institute Of Health/World Health Organisation
The organization issues guidelines regarding the management of hypertension in the context of ACS.

best life expectancy,dietary pattern	Japanese Diet
Mediterranean Diet
Both the Mediterranean and Japanese diets are associated with the world's best life expectancy.

health assessment,obesity measurement	Body Mass Index
World Health Organisation
The World Health Organisation recommends using the Body Mass Index for assessing the degree of obesity.

physiological correlation,risk factor	Central Obesity
Hypertension
Central obesity is associated with high prevalence of hypertension and lipid abnormalities.

Symptomatic treatment,high-risk indicator	Hypertension, Tachycardia
Intravenous Beta-blockers
Intravenous beta-blockers are indicated when significant hypertension, tachycardia, or other symptoms are present.

nutritional guidance,secondary prevention	Monounsaturated Fats
N-3-omega Fatty Acids
N-3-omega fatty acids are listed as components of cardioprotective diets.

antiplatelet management,combination therapy	Aspirin, Heparin
Glycoprotein IIb/IIIa Receptor Antagonists
Use of GP IIb/IIIa antagonists should be combined with aspirin and heparin for suspected non-ST elevation ACS.

disease risk,elevated risk	Coronary Heart Disease
Type 1 Diabetes
Type 1 diabetes is associated with a marked increase of coronary heart disease.

assessment method,clinical recommendation	Obesity
Waist Circumference
Waist circumference is recommended as a useful index of obesity for clinical assessment.

cardiac health,risk factor control	Secondary Prevention
Smoking, Hyperlipidemia
The secondary prevention of ACS includes controlling cardiac risk factors such as smoking and hyperlipidemia.

dietary component,nutritional content	Mediterranean Diet
Monounsaturated Fat
Monounsaturated fat is specifically emphasized as a component of the Mediterranean diet.

measurement tool,risk assessment	BMI Classification
Body Mass Index
The Body Mass Index is evaluated against specific classification grades to determine the degree of obesity.

pathology link,risk increase	Central Obesity
Coronary Heart Disease
Central obesity increases the risk of coronary artery disease.

increased risk,public health hazard	Passive Smoking
Smoking
Passive smoking is noted as increasing the risk of cardiovascular disease.

characteristic pattern,risk factor	Impaired Glucose Tolerance
Type 2 Diabetes
IGT is associated with a cardiovascular risk pattern characteristic of type 2 diabetes.

correlation,health risk	Central Obesity
Lipid Abnormalities
Central obesity is associated with a high prevalence of lipid abnormalities.

death cause,risk factor	Coronary Heart Disease
Smoking
Smoking is responsible for cardiovascular death, increasing the risk of coronary heart disease.

calculation standard,measurement metric	Body Mass Index
Body Weight
Body mass index calculation requires body weight in kilograms and height in meters.

clinical management,risk factor	Blood Pressure
Coronary Heart Disease
Elevated blood pressure is a significant risk factor for coronary heart disease, requiring comprehensive risk assessment.

etiology,risk association	Coronary Heart Disease
Risk Factor
Risk factors are independently related to the subsequent occurrence of coronary heart disease.

data.src_id: Non St Elevation Acute Coronary Syndrome
data.content:
Type,inclusion	Non St Elevation Acute Coronary Syndrome
Unstable Angina
Unstable angina is listed as one of the types of acute coronary syndromes encompassed by Non ST elevation acute coronary syndrome.

detection strategy,prevention	Coronary Heart Disease
Screening
Screening is necessary for a coronary artery disease prevention program to reduce clinical events and morbidity.

Diagnosis,differentiation	Non-Ischaemic Causes Of Pain
Physical Examination
Physical examination is specifically useful for diagnosing non-ischaemic causes of pain that mimic ACS symptoms.

Diagnostic utility,assessment	Complete Blood Count
Renal Function Tests
CBC and renal function tests are jointly useful for diagnosing symptoms and evaluating concurrent medical conditions.

development retardant,preventive measure	Coronary Heart Disease
Lifestyle Modification
Lifestyle modification can retard the development of coronary heart disease.

data.tgt_id: Non St Elevation Acute Coronary Syndrome
data.content:
Diagnostic cornerstone,risk stratification	ECG Analysis
Non St Elevation Acute Coronary Syndrome
ECG analysis is identified as a primary, essential diagnostic test for risk stratification in the syndrome.

models,theory proposal	Diclemente
Procjaska
Procjaska and Diclemente are the authors who proposed the Stages of Change Model.

dietary recommendation,life expectancy	Coronary Heart Disease
Mediterranean Diet
The Mediterranean diet is associated with the best life expectancy in the world concerning coronary health.

assessment,theoretical framework	Behavioural Change
Stages Of Change Model
The Stages of Change Model is designed to assess individual readiness for behavioral change.

Diagnosis,Evaluation	Chronic Ischaemic Heart Disease
Exercise Stress Testing (EST)
EST is an informative examination particularly useful for the detection and quantification of chronic ischaemic heart disease.

dietary recommendation,life expectancy	Coronary Heart Disease
Japanese Diet
The Japanese diet is associated with the best life expectancy in the world concerning coronary health.

low intention,stage	Behavioural Change
Pre-Contemplation
Pre-contemplation is a stage where individuals do not intend to change their high-risk behavior.

Symptom Management,Treatment	Angina Pectoris
Beta-Blockers
Beta-blockers are effective in reducing angina pectoris and delaying the onset of angina.

data.tgt_id: Ministry Of Health And Quality Of Life/Mauritius Institute Of Health/World Health Organisation
data.content:
guideline origin,risk management	Cardiovascular Disease
Ministry Of Health And Quality Of Life/Mauritius Institute Of Health/World Health Organisation
The organization published comprehensive guidelines detailing risk factor reduction for cardiovascular disease.

ambivalence,stage	Behavioural Change
Contemplation
Contemplation is a stage where individuals seriously intend to change but experience considerable ambivalence.

Cornerstone,Management	Chronic Ischaemic Heart Disease
Medical Therapy
Medical therapy combined with aggressive cardiovascular risk factor modification is the cornerstone of IHD treatment.

information source,public health	Coronary Heart Disease
World Health Organisation
The World Health Organisation provides health information and guidelines regarding coronary heart disease.

Diagnosis,Differential Diagnosis	Chest Pain
EST
EST is used to evaluate patients with symptoms suggestive of chest pain.

data.src_id: Ministry Of Health And Quality Of Life/Mauritius Institute Of Health/World Health Organisation
data.content:
guideline source,prevention focus	Ministry Of Health And Quality Of Life/Mauritius Institute Of Health/World Health Organisation
Non-Communicable Disease
The organization's guidelines cover the scope of non-communicable diseases.

cessation,risk factor control	Primary Prevention
Smoking
The guidelines strongly encourage patient and family cessation of smoking as a primary preventative measure.

intervention recommendation,lifestyle modification	Cardiovascular Disease
Physical Activity
Physical activity is highly encouraged for managing cardiovascular disease risk.

Mortality Reduction,Treatment	Beta-Blockers
Heart Failure
Beta-blockers have been shown to reduce total mortality in patients with compensated heart failure.

data.tgt_id: Ministry Of Health And Quality Of Life/Mauritius Institute Of Health
data.content:
health guideline,source attribution	Coronary Heart Disease
Ministry Of Health And Quality Of Life/Mauritius Institute Of Health
Ministry of Health and Quality of Life/Mauritius Institute of Health provides health information and guidelines regarding coronary heart disease.

lifestyle modification,management goals	Blood Pressure Control
Hypertension
Blood pressure control requires lifestyle modification and drug management to keep readings below specific thresholds.

coexisting condition,treatment requirement	Diabetes
Lipid Management
Diabetes management requires achieving near normal FPG alongside managing other risks.

glucose control,intervention recommendation	Cardiovascular Disease
Weight Management
Weight management is crucial for controlling risk factors like high blood pressure, elevated triglycerides, and glucose levels.

individualized care,therapeutic target	Blood Pressure Control
Blood Pressure Readings
Blood pressure control requires individualized drug management to ensure readings are consistently at or below 140/90 mmHg.

quantifiable goal,treatment assessment	LDL Cholesterol
Lipid Management
LDL cholesterol levels are measured against specific goals (e.g., < 100 mg/dl) to guide drug therapy decisions.

blood clotting management,monitoring metric	Antiplatelets/Anticoagulants
International Normalized Ratio
INR must be managed to maintain a ratio of 2 to 3.5 for patients taking warfarin post-myocardial infarction (MI).

quantitative threshold,risk factor assessment	HDL Cholesterol
Lipid Management
HDL cholesterol must be greater than 35 mg/dl for optimal lipid management.

diagnosis,initial management	Chronic Ischaemic Heart Disease
Primary Care Physician
The primary care physician performs the preliminary diagnosis and management of chronic ischaemic heart disease.

combination therapy,drug recommendation	Lipid Management
Statin Drug Therapy
Statin drug therapy is indicated for lipid management, potentially in combination if the LDL goal is not met.

initial presentation,symptom manifestation	Angina Pectoris
Chronic Ischaemic Heart Disease
Angina pectoris is identified as the most common initial symptom of chronic ischaemic heart disease.

initial referral,screening	Acute Coronary Syndrome
Primary Care Physician
Patients with ACS should be referred to a cardiologist.

drug recommendation,triglyceride management	Fibrate Drug Therapy
Lipid Management
Fibrate therapy is recommended for lipid management, particularly addressing high triglyceride levels.

risk factor modification,treatment regimen	Chronic Ischaemic Heart Disease
Exercise
A regular exercise program is recommended as part of a multifactorial intervention program for chronic ischaemic heart disease.

diet restrictions,therapeutic goal	Cardiovascular Disease
Lipid Management
Lipid management sets specific goals for LDL, HDL, and triglycerides, requiring diet and drug therapy.

patient specific,risk factor consideration	Cardiovascular Disease
Oestrogen Replacement Therapy
Oestrogen replacement therapy is specifically suggested for post-menopausal women with multiple CVD risk factors.

combination therapy,drug recommendation	Lipid Management
Niacin Drug Therapy
Niacin therapy is suggested for lipid management, sometimes combined with statin therapy.
